title: NDA Exam 2026
meta_description: NDA Exam 2026 — National Defence Academy entrance for Army, Navy, Air Force after 12th. Exam pattern, physical standards, salary, UPSC notification dates, and preparation tips.

hero.one_liner: Gateway to Indian Armed Forces as an officer — join Army, Navy, or Air Force through India's premier defence training academy after 12th

what_is_it: The National Defence Academy (NDA) exam is conducted by UPSC twice a year (NDA I and NDA II) to recruit candidates for the NDA and Naval Academy courses. NDA is the premier tri-service training academy located in Khadakwasla, Pune, where cadets are trained for 3 years before being commissioned as officers in the Indian Army, Navy, or Air Force.

The exam is open to 12th pass or appearing candidates aged 16.5-19.5 years. For Army wing, any stream (Arts/Science/Commerce) works. For Navy and Air Force, 12th with Physics and Mathematics is mandatory. After clearing the written exam (conducted by UPSC), candidates appear for SSB Interview (5-day assessment) which tests personality, officer-like qualities, and leadership potential.

NDA is considered one of the most prestigious career paths for young Indians. Officers commissioned through NDA are highly respected and receive excellent training, salary, and perks. Starting salary after training is approximately ₹56,100 basic + allowances = ₹70,000-90,000/month, with free accommodation, medical, canteen, and other facilities.
NDA I 2026 exam is scheduled for 13 April 2026. The notification is typically released 3-4 months before the exam date.

key_details.label: Education
key_details.value: 12th pass or appearing. Army: any stream. Navy/Air Force: 12th with Physics + Maths.

key_details.label: Age
key_details.value: 16.5 to 19.5 years (born between specific dates mentioned in notification)

key_details.label: Gender
key_details.value: Male AND Female candidates eligible (women admitted from 2023 batch onwards)

key_details.label: Selection
key_details.value: Written Exam (UPSC) → SSB Interview (5 days) → Medical Examination → Merit List

key_details.label: Training
key_details.value: 3 years at NDA Khadakwasla + 1 year at respective service academy (IMA/INA/AFA)

exam_pattern.description: Two papers conducted on the same day. Paper 1 is Mathematics (300 marks) and Paper 2 is General Ability Test (600 marks). Total 900 marks. Only candidates clearing the written cutoff are called for SSB Interview.

extra_sections.heading: SSB Interview — The Real Challenge
content: The SSB (Services Selection Board) Interview is a 5-day assessment that tests whether you have 'Officer Like Qualities (OLQs)'. It's not a traditional interview — it's a comprehensive personality assessment conducted by psychologists and military officers.
Day 1 — Screening: Officer Intelligence Rating (OIR) test + Picture Perception and Description Test (PPDT) + group discussion on the story you wrote. About 50-60% are screened out on Day 1.

Day 2 — Psychology Tests: Thematic Apperception Test (TAT — write stories on pictures), Word Association Test (WAT — write sentences for 60 words in 15 seconds each), Situation Reaction Test (SRT — respond to 60 situations), and Self Description Test (SD).

Day 3 & 4 — Group Testing Officer (GTO) tasks: Group Discussion, Group Planning Exercise, Progressive Group Task, Half Group Task, Individual Obstacles, Command Task, and Final Group Task. These are outdoor activities testing leadership, teamwork, and problem-solving.

Day 5 — Personal Interview with the Interviewing Officer (IO). Questions about your background, interests, family, current affairs, and motivation for joining defence forces. Conference with all three assessors — final recommendation.

About 1 in 6-8 candidates who appear for SSB get recommended. SSB preparation is as important as written exam preparation. Read 'Let's Crack SSB Interview' by Dr. Arth Chopra for comprehensive guidance.

extra_sections.heading: NDA vs CDS vs Agnipath — Which Path to Choose?

preparation_tips: Mathematics paper (300 marks) is the game-changer. If you're weak in Math, NDA will be very tough. Cover: Algebra, Matrices, Trigonometry, Calculus (basics), Analytical Geometry, Vector Algebra, Statistics, and Probability. Use NCERT 11th-12th Math textbooks as primary resource.

General Ability Test Paper 2 has two sections: English (200 marks) and General Knowledge (400 marks). GK covers Physics, Chemistry, Biology, History, Geography, Current Affairs, and Defence-related topics. Focus on NCERT 6-12 for science and social studies.

Start SSB preparation from Day 1 — don't wait till after the written exam. Read newspapers daily, develop opinions on national/international issues, practice group discussions with friends, and work on your physical fitness. SSB evaluates your personality over 5 days — it can't be faked.

Physical fitness is essential — not for the written exam but for SSB (obstacle course, outdoor tasks) and the 3-year NDA training which is very physically demanding. Start running, swimming, and body-weight exercises now.

faqs.question: Can girls apply for NDA?
faqs.answer: Yes. The Supreme Court ordered women's admission to NDA in 2021, and female candidates have been appearing since 2022. Women can join the Army and Air Force wings through NDA. The training and curriculum are the same.

faqs.question: What degree do you get at NDA?
faqs.answer: NDA cadets earn a Bachelor's degree from Jawaharlal Nehru University (JNU) during their 3-year training. Army cadets get BA/BSc. Navy and Air Force cadets get BSc. The degree is fully recognized and equivalent to any regular university degree.

faqs.question: What if I clear the written exam but fail SSB?
faqs.answer: You can appear again in the next NDA exam cycle (NDA I or NDA II) as long as you meet the age criteria. Many successful NDA candidates clear SSB on their 2nd or 3rd attempt. Each SSB attempt teaches you something and improves your chances.

faqs.question: NDA salary after training?
faqs.answer: After 4 years of training (3 NDA + 1 academy), you're commissioned as Lieutenant at Level 10 — basic pay ₹56,100/month + DA + Military Service Pay + Transport Allowance + other allowances = approximately ₹80,000-90,000/month. Plus free accommodation in military cantonment, medical for family, canteen facilities, and subsidized education for children.

faqs.question: I'm 19 and in final year of graduation. NDA or CDS?
faqs.answer: If you're below 19.5 years, you're still eligible for NDA. However, NDA means leaving college and starting 3-year training (you'll get a degree at NDA). CDS allows you to complete your graduation first and then join for shorter training (1.5 years). If you value your current degree program, wait for CDS after graduation. If you want the full NDA experience (most prestigious entry), apply immediately.
